name: The gospel and the prophets (Romans 10)
notes: In Romans 10 Paul gathers a chain of Old Testament texts to trace the preaching of the gospel and Israel's response to it. He cites Isaiah 52:7 on the beautiful feet of those who bring glad tidings of peace, and Psalm 19:4 to show that their sound has gone into all the earth. He then turns to Israel's accountability, quoting Moses in Deuteronomy 32:21 on being provoked to jealousy by a foolish nation, and Isaiah 65:1-2 on God being found of them that sought him not while he stretches forth his hands all the day to a disobedient people. The shared core is the apostolic gospel reaching the Gentiles and leaving Israel without excuse. The labels mark the exact KJV wording Paul carries over from each prophet; where his citation rephrases the source (Isaiah's mountains, Psalm 19's line) the differing diction is left as a matter for further study.

links.label: How beautiful ... the feet of ... peace
found of them that sought me not
